wedge_anchor: Breast Cancer Survivors (BCS) in first 24 months post-active-treatment, PT senology / oncology follow-up
thesis_anchor: BT-03 (PT private oncology white-label) primary; BT-04 (IPO + EU-funded research) parallel; BT-02 (pharma) and BT-01 (DiGA) downstream expansion

metadata.research_method: WebSearch budget not used in this run; entries assembled from prior strategic report + sector knowledge. Every entry where program existence / URL / cycle could not be web-confirmed has explicit open_issues flag per framework principle 7.

partners.name: CUF Oncologia — Centro de Senologia (CUF Descobertas / CUF Tejo)

relevant_program.name: CUF Centro de Senologia (multidisciplinary breast unit, includes survivorship follow-up)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: rolling — JMS innovation pipeline runs continuously; no public cycle for digital-health pilots
relevant_program.focus: Diagnosis, treatment, survivorship follow-up of breast cancer; psycho-oncology embedded in some units

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Largest PT private senology footprint with explicit BCS survivorship pathway and dedicated psycho-oncology slots. Buyer (service-line director) has the exact pain BT-03 monetizes: <20% psych-support coverage in cohort with 30-40% clinical anxiety/depression.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Winning one CUF unit unlocks the José de Mello Saúde group (~17 hospitals/clinics) — pure BT-03 multiplication. Dataset feeds BT-01 DiGA and BT-02 pharma.

partners.what_we_offer_them: Between-visit visibility on the BCS cohort the senology director already feels they are losing. AUC 0.78 depression-risk flag [EV-043] surfaces the 1-2 patients/month a clinician should call back. Reduces escalation to ER/external psychiatry and protects the €2-5K/yr private follow-up revenue per BCS patient. Fits a clinical-quality KPI dashboard the director can show the group medical board.
partners.what_we_ask_them: 12-week PoV at one unit (CUF Descobertas or CUF Tejo): N=20-30 BCS, 1 senologist + 1 psycho-oncologist, signed LOI stating willingness to subscribe at >=200 EUR/clinician/month [EV-047] if PoV hits >=70% data completeness.

contact_path.preferred_channel: warm intro via existing PerSense clinical PIs at the 2 hospitals already deployed [EV-043]; secondary: direct to CUF Oncologia service-line director

open_issues: Specific named CUF senology director / psycho-oncology coordinator not in evidence ledger — needs LinkedIn / direct call to identify.
No public CUF digital-health pilot URL; deadline/cycle is rolling but unconfirmed.
Procurement cycle in JMS group can be 3-9 months — the LOI estimate assumes department-level, not group-level, sign-off.
Whether 200 EUR/clinician/mo is floor or ceiling at CUF is not benchmarked.
partners.next_action: This week: ask the existing PerSense PIs to nominate the most senior senology contact at CUF Descobertas; draft a 1-page PoV brief framed around 'between-visit BCS visibility for 12 weeks'.

partners.name: IPO Lisboa Francisco Gentil — Servico de Psico-Oncologia / Unidade de Mama

relevant_program.name: Unidade de Mama + Servico de Psico-Oncologia (institutional survivorship cohort)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: rolling research-collaboration submissions; FCT/COMPETE 2030 cycles annual
relevant_program.focus: Largest PT BCS institutional cohort; active research and survivorship clinic

fit_to_thesis.rationale: IPO Lisboa is the canonical PT BCS institutional buyer for BT-04 (research-services + grant-funded). Psycho-oncology service exists but is under-resourced relative to cohort size — the exact gap statement.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Winning IPO Lisboa creates a credibility chain to IPO Porto and IPO Coimbra (inter-IPO research network) and unlocks FCT/COMPETE 2030 / Horizon Mission Cancer consortium roles. Plus: IPO endpoint data is the credibility seed for BT-01 DiGA.

partners.what_we_offer_them: A turnkey BCS digital-survivorship cohort that makes the PI competitive for FCT/EIC/Horizon Mission Cancer calls without consuming clinician time. PerSense brings 5-10K EUR pilot cost vs 120K iNNOVSensing precedent [EV-046, EV-048]. Co-authorship + dataset access for the psycho-oncology lead.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Institutional research-collaboration agreement covering a 12-week PoV (N=30-40 BCS), with named PI in psycho-oncology + senology, plus letter of support for an FCT/COMPETE 2030 consortium application within 6 months.

contact_path.preferred_channel: direct to Servico de Psico-Oncologia coordinator + Unidade de Mama director; parallel via Conselho de Administracao for institutional cover

open_issues: Named IPO Lisboa psycho-oncology coordinator not in ledger — outreach must identify.
IPO IRB / ethics cycles historically 3-6 months; LOI estimate may slip.
Conversion path from research-services to a recurring license post-grant is unproven for IPO.
Whether IPO Lisboa would expect the tool gratis as 'academic deliverable' is unanswered.
partners.next_action: This week: send a 2-page institutional brief framed as 'BCS survivorship FCT/Horizon-readiness pilot' to the Servico de Psico-Oncologia secretariat; cc Unidade de Mama.

partners.name: Lusiadas Saude — Centro Mama / Oncologia (Lusiadas Lisboa, Lusiadas Porto)

relevant_program.name: Lusiadas Centro Mama (multidisciplinary breast unit)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: rolling; no public digital-health partnership cycle published
relevant_program.focus: Senology + oncology + survivorship follow-up; smaller scale than CUF but more concentrated decision-making

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Direct BT-03 buyer; Lusiadas has historically moved faster than CUF on individual-unit pilots due to flatter decision structure. Same buyer pain (BCS psycho-oncology gap) and same purchase model (department subscription).
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Lusiadas group ~14 units; one unit unlocks group.

partners.what_we_offer_them: Differentiated 'continuous survivorship care' service line that Lusiadas can market as a competitive feature vs CUF/Luz Saude. Clinician-retention angle: psycho-oncologist load is lighter when JITAI flags triage.
partners.what_we_ask_them: 12-week PoV at Lusiadas Lisboa Centro Mama; LOI at >=200 EUR/clinician/mo conditional on data-completeness >=70%. Preference: combined LOI with CUF as parallel, so PoV is multi-site from day 1.

contact_path.preferred_channel: direct outreach to Centro Mama clinical lead + Oncologia service-line director

open_issues: Named clinical lead at Lusiadas Centro Mama not in ledger.
Whether Lusiadas runs psycho-oncology in-house or via referral is uncertain — affects buyer identity.
Group-level digital-health gatekeeper (CIO / Innovation) not mapped.
partners.next_action: This week: LinkedIn search for Lusiadas Centro Mama coordinator + cold email with the same 1-page PoV brief used for CUF.

partners.name: Hospital da Luz — Centro Clinico da Mama (Luz Saude / Fidelidade)

relevant_program.name: Centro Clinico da Mama, Hospital da Luz Lisboa
relevant_program.focus: Multidisciplinary breast cancer center with survivorship and psycho-oncology consults

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Third major PT private group with named breast unit. Owned by Fidelidade — natural bridge to Multicare insurer (BT-05) once BT-03 lands.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-03 -> BT-05 cross-sell: Luz Saude + Multicare common ownership creates a single buyer that could subscribe both clinically (hospital seat) and as insurer rider (PMPM).

partners.what_we_offer_them: Same dashboard-and-flag value as CUF/Lusiadas, plus an explicit Multicare-channel pitch: 'a benefit your owner-insurer can underwrite'.
partners.what_we_ask_them: 12-week PoV at Centro Clinico da Mama Hospital da Luz Lisboa; LOI at >=200 EUR/clinician/mo plus an exploratory meeting with Multicare oncology product team.

contact_path.preferred_channel: direct to Centro Clinico da Mama coordinator; secondary: Luz Saude Innovation / Digital Health

open_issues: Named coordinator unknown.
Whether Luz has a formal digital-health innovation function with budget authority is unclear.
Coordination Luz<->Multicare for the dual-pitch is hypothesized, not validated.
partners.next_action: Identify Centro Clinico da Mama medical coordinator; queue email after CUF/Lusiadas first-contact wave (week 2).

partners.name: Trofa Saude — Oncologia (Norte)

relevant_program.name: Trofa Saude Oncologia (regional Norte network)
relevant_program.focus: Oncology service across Norte hospitals; senology presence varies by site

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Smaller BCS volume than the big-3, but Norte regional concentration complements Lisboa-centric CUF/Lusiadas/Luz Saude — geographical breadth for a multi-site PoV story.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-03 PT private expansion; modest multiplier.

partners.what_we_offer_them: A way to compete with Sao Joao / IPO Porto on continuity of survivorship care; small-group decision speed.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Light-touch single-site 12-week PoV; LOI conditional on PoV success.

contact_path.preferred_channel: direct email to Oncologia coordinator

open_issues: Whether Trofa Saude has a dedicated breast unit with psycho-oncology capacity not confirmed.
BCS cohort volume per Trofa unit unknown.
partners.next_action: Defer to wave 2 unless a warm intro emerges.

partners.name: IPO Porto — Servico de Psico-Oncologia / Clinica de Mama

relevant_program.name: Clinica de Mama IPO Porto + Psico-Oncologia
relevant_program.focus: Major Norte BCS institutional cohort; active in EU oncology research consortia

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Parallel to IPO Lisboa for BT-04. Often a faster mover on research collaborations than IPO Lisboa.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Inter-IPO network (Lisboa-Porto-Coimbra). One IPO LOI is the lever for the other two within 3-6 months. Strong Horizon EU consortium eligibility.

partners.what_we_offer_them: FCT/Horizon Mission Cancer-readiness data pack; co-authorship; dataset asset for the psycho-oncology research line.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Institutional collaboration agreement for 12-week PoV; commit to co-PI on next FCT call.

contact_path.preferred_channel: direct to Psico-Oncologia + Clinica de Mama coordinators

open_issues: Named psycho-oncology PI not in ledger.
Coordination overhead if running IPO Lisboa + IPO Porto in parallel may strain the 4-FTE PerSense team.
partners.next_action: Send the institutional brief in parallel with IPO Lisboa; mark internally which lands first.

partners.name: IPO Coimbra — Servico de Psico-Oncologia

relevant_program.name: IPO Coimbra Senologia + Psico-Oncologia
relevant_program.focus: Centro region BCS cohort; smaller than Lisboa/Porto but with strong research links to FMUC / CHUC

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Completes the inter-IPO triangle. Lower priority while IPO Lisboa and Porto are active.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Bridges to CHUC and FMUC academic research.

partners.what_we_offer_them: Membership in the BCS-survivorship FCT consortium PerSense is anchoring with IPO Lisboa/Porto.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Letter of support to join the consortium; PoV optional in wave 2.

contact_path.preferred_channel: direct via Psico-Oncologia coordinator

open_issues: PI not identified.
Adding a third IPO may slow the consortium more than it accelerates.
partners.next_action: Wave 2 outreach; include in FCT consortium pitch only after IPO Lisboa/Porto signal.

partners.name: Liga Portuguesa Contra o Cancro — Movimento Vencer e Viver / Nucleos Regionais

relevant_program.name: Movimento Vencer e Viver (national BCS peer-support program); Nucleos Regionais Norte/Centro/Sul
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: ongoing peer-support program; periodic project calls
relevant_program.focus: Largest BCS peer-support and advocacy network in PT; convenes survivors for support groups, education, advocacy

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Direct end-user channel to the BCS cohort across PT; legitimacy with patients (recruitment + ethics framing) and a credible patient-voice partner for any DiGA dossier later.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: LPCC endorsement is the patient-advocacy pillar that BT-01 DiGA dossiers and BT-02 pharma RWE programs both demand.

partners.what_we_offer_them: A digital adjunct that supports their own mission (mental-health between visits) without requiring LPCC volunteer time. Co-branded data on BCS survivorship distress that LPCC can use in advocacy with DGS.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Endorsement letter; help recruiting BCS for PoV across nucleos regionais; future co-authorship on patient-experience publications.

contact_path.preferred_channel: direct via LPCC Direcao Nacional + Movimento Vencer e Viver coordinator

open_issues: LPCC's policy on digital-health endorsements not confirmed — may require LPCC ethics review.
Movimento Vencer e Viver coordinator name not in ledger.
Whether endorsement = recruitment access is uncertain.
partners.next_action: Email LPCC Direcao Nacional with a 1-page patient-impact brief and request a 30-min meeting.

partners.name: Champalimaud Foundation — Breast Unit / Champalimaud Clinical Centre

relevant_program.name: Breast Unit, Champalimaud Clinical Centre
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: rolling research collaborations; internal innovation funds periodic
relevant_program.focus: Highly specialized BCS treatment + research; international referral patients

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Prestigious anchor for evidence credibility (DiGA dossier later), but cohort is smaller and more affluent — not representative of the PT BCS volume play.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Champalimaud halo helps BT-01 DiGA narrative and BT-02 pharma framing more than BT-03 hospital revenue.

partners.what_we_offer_them: Co-authored publication on BCS digital survivorship with PerSense AUC 0.78 model; visibility of Champalimaud Breast Unit as adopter in international DTx narratives.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Pilot site (smaller N) framed as scientific collaboration; co-authorship.

contact_path.preferred_channel: via existing Lisboa academic network; direct to Breast Unit Director

open_issues: Champalimaud's affluent international cohort may not match the BT-03 buyer pain.
Internal innovation funding cycles not public.
partners.next_action: Lower priority than CUF/Lusiadas; queue as 'evidence credibility partner' for DiGA Phase-2.

fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: CHULN-FMUL-iMM triangle; Horizon Mission Cancer + ENESM-aligned positioning.

partners.what_we_offer_them: Research collaboration that supports the senology unit's academic output; turnkey BCS digital-survivorship cohort for FMUL theses; FCT-readiness.
partners.what_we_ask_them: PoV at the Unidade de Patologia Mamaria (N=20-30); IRB submission; co-PI from FMUL / iMM.

contact_path.preferred_channel: via FMUL / NOVA Medical academic network

open_issues: IRB at CHULN can be 4-6 months — may slip the PoV timeline.
Named coordinator unknown.
Whether existing PerSense 2-hospital deployment overlaps with CHULN [EV-043] is unspecified.
partners.next_action: Confirm whether one of PerSense's existing 2 hospital sites is CHULN — if yes, big shortcut.

fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: CHUSJ-FMUP-i3S-CINTESIS-ISPUP cluster — one of the densest research multipliers in PT.

partners.what_we_offer_them: Same FCT/Horizon-readiness pitch as IPO; appeals to institutional KPI of grant capture.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Institutional research collaboration; co-PI from FMUP psico-oncologia; PoV at Unidade de Mama.

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Completes geographic triangle (Lisboa/Norte/Centro) for grant-narrative. Lower BCS volume than the other two.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: FMUC + IPO Coimbra + CHUC bundle for the Centro consortium leg.

partners.name: ISPUP — Instituto de Saude Publica UPorto (Psycho-Oncology / Cancer Epi line)

fit_to_thesis.rationale: ISPUP holds the canonical PT BCS QoL/distress evidence and method credibility — co-authorship strengthens DiGA narrative and de-risks BT-04 grant applications.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: ISPUP is the bridge between IPO Porto + CHUSJ + i3S + CINTESIS — all four become more reachable through ISPUP.

partners.what_we_offer_them: Methodologically-rich BCS-cohort dataset (EMA + sensor + AUC 0.78 model) that complements ISPUP's existing QoL questionnaires; strong co-publication potential.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Methodological co-PI on PoV; co-application on FCT/Horizon Mission Cancer; ethics review support.

fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Bridges into Nova SBE HEM and into IPO Lisboa via shared research clusters.

partners.what_we_offer_them: Cohort + methodology partnership for FCT/Horizon Mission Cancer.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Co-PI; ethics-pathway support.

open_issues: Specific BCS line at CHRC not confirmed.

partners.name: Roche Portugal — Medical Affairs (Breast Oncology)

relevant_program.name: Roche Medical Affairs / Real-World Evidence programs (BCS-relevant: Phesgo, Kadcyla, Perjeta)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: ongoing investigator-initiated and medical-affairs RWE budgets; annual cycle
relevant_program.focus: Largest breast-oncology pharma in PT; active in adjuvant/early-breast-cancer survivorship outcomes

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Roche PT Medical Affairs is the most plausible first pharma anchor for BT-02. BCS adjuvant adherence + affective trajectories on Phesgo/Kadcyla is an unsolved RWE gap.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: One Roche PT contract = template for Roche EU; bridges to BT-02 multi-country expansion.

partners.what_we_offer_them: BCS-specific RWE on adherence + affective state at 3-5x lower cost than CRO equivalent. AUC 0.78 depression model adds an actuarial-style claim Roche MA finds appealing.
partners.what_we_ask_them: 40-80K EUR initial scoping contract; named MA champion; introduction to global BCS franchise.

contact_path.preferred_channel: via PT oncology KOL network (IPO Lisboa, Champalimaud); secondary direct

open_issues: No documented Roche relationship in PerSense.
Pharma exclusivity asks could lock out BT-01 DiGA path.
PT MA budget for RWE not benchmarked.
partners.next_action: Hold for Phase-2 of BT-03 (after first hospital LOI). Use that LOI as the credibility door-opener with Roche MA.

partners.name: Siemens Healthineers Portugal — Digital Health / Oncology imaging adjacency

relevant_program.name: Siemens Healthineers Innovation Think Tank / digital-health pilot framework
relevant_program.focus: Digital-health innovation, particularly imaging-led oncology workflows

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Siemens Healthineers' BCS adjacency is in imaging/diagnosis, not psychosocial follow-up — weak fit. Corporate philanthropy / employee-wellness angle (women's health) is plausible but not directly BCS-monetizable.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Imaging->follow-up data integration is a long-horizon expansion vector, not a PoV partner.

partners.what_we_offer_them: A women's-health digital-companion narrative for corporate-philanthropy or innovation-think-tank visibility.
partners.what_we_ask_them: Innovation-track cohort participation; in-kind compute / cloud / IoT support.

contact_path.preferred_channel: via BIG Impact + Siemens PT

open_issues: BCS fit for Siemens is structurally weak.
Innovation Think Tank PT presence not confirmed.
Risk of forced-fit narrative to honor BIG Impact partner list.
partners.next_action: Keep on the map for BIG Impact mandate; do not invest sales cycles unless a specific imaging-follow-up integration emerges.

fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: PT insurer template -> ES insurers (Sanitas, Adeslas).

Insurer sales cycles 12+ months — not aligned with PoV urgency.

partners.name: Sociedade Portuguesa de Senologia (SPS)

relevant_program.name: SPS scientific society — annual congress + clinical-guideline working groups
relevant_program.focus: Largest PT senology professional body — clinical-guideline credibility for survivorship pathways

fit_to_thesis.rationale: SPS endorsement carries weight with private-hospital senology directors (BT-03 buyers). Congress visibility = warm-intro factory.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Once endorsed, SPS connections accelerate cross-hospital BT-03 expansion. Also opens OPP psycho-oncology section dialogue.
